notes: These are local, benchmark-anchored target bands for Stage 3 calibration.
They are not claims of editorial truth or a solved NYT style model.
The purpose is to center style analysis on semantic-majority benchmark behavior and make inflation visible.

unique_group_type_count.description: Benchmark holdout boards are usually semantic-majority rather than four-way mixed.

semantic_group_count.description: Semantic-majority is the default healthy editorial prior.

theme_group_count.description: Theme groups are allowed but rare in the benchmark center.

phonetic_group_count.description: Phonetic groups should remain uncommon showcase mechanisms.

wordplay_group_count.description: Wordplay is acceptable, but benchmark boards usually have zero or one wordplay group.

label_token_mean.description: Category labels should feel natural and benchmark-like, not machine-short by default.

label_token_std.description: Label shape can vary naturally across semantic-majority boards.

evidence_interpretability.description: Groups should expose explicit evidence and understandable rule provenance.

semantic_wordplay_balance.description: Benchmark holdout boards skew strongly toward semantic mass.

coherence_trickiness_balance.description: Benchmark boards are often more straightforward than machine-mixed boards.

ambiguity_score.description: High ambiguity remains undesirable even when style looks promising.

earned_wordplay_score.description: Earned wordplay is allowed but should not dominate the board prior.

editorial_flatness_score.description: Flat, mechanism-first boards should sit outside the benchmark center.

family_repetition_risk.description: Benchmark-centered boards should not look like repeated machine families.

family_saturation.description: Family saturation should stay low when the run is editorially healthy.

formulaic_mix_score.description: Formulaic mixed templates should land outside the target center.

microtheme_smallness.description: Tiny curated themes should be rare rather than a default ingredient.

redundancy_score.description: Redundancy here reflects natural mechanism concentration in semantic-majority benchmark boards.

surface_wordplay_score.description: Surface-only lexical and phonetic families should rarely define the board.

style_alignment_score.description: Boards should land near the benchmark-centered Stage 3 style bands without trivial inflation.
